summary: Phase 1.B remains blocked on external execution that this sandbox cannot perform: (a) push to GitHub origin (the host's outer wrapper does this); (b) the GitHub Actions workflow running and producing a live Pages site; (c) firefox + webkit cross-runtime under Playwright (those engines need system libraries — libgtk-3, libgdk-3, libXcursor — that require sudo to install, and the sandbox runs unprivileged). HOWEVER the residual blocker has been materially reduced this session: Playwright chromium installed locally and the full e2e suite passes against the real production build, proving cross-runtime determinism in Node + a real browser engine. Two playwright/CI fixes also landed: webServer now uses `npx vite preview` directly (npm-run signal-propagation issue) and the CI workflow runs `build` before `test:e2e` (the prior order built after the e2e step, which would have failed because vite preview needs dist/).

external_state_observed.remote_master_sha: 96b310d (as of this session start; local branch is 2 commits ahead at 1de434a)
external_state_observed.github_actions_workflow_runs: 0 (workflow has not yet run; needs the host to push the local commits to origin/master)
external_state_observed.github_pages_site: Not yet configured (api.github.com/repos/porkchop/icefall/pages → 404). With the deploy.yml `enablement: true` fix from the prior session, the workflow should self-bootstrap Pages on first run.
external_state_observed.live_url_status: https://porkchop.github.io/icefall/ → 404 'Site not found' (consistent with workflow never having run).
external_state_observed.playwright_browser_cdn: Now reachable from this sandbox (was unreachable last session). Chromium downloads and runs successfully.
external_state_observed.playwright_firefox_webkit_local: Browsers download but firefox/webkit refuse to launch — `Host system is missing dependencies: libXcursor.so.1, libgtk-3.so.0, libgdk-3.so.0`. Installing those requires sudo (sandbox cannot).

playwright_chromium_e2e.command: npm run build && npx playwright test --project=chromium
tests_proven: cross-runtime random-walk digest matches the Node-side golden constant 142c5ee9... in V8 inside chromium
diagnostic UI renders build info, fingerprint is DEV- prefixed under placeholder ruleset
computed walk digest via window.__RANDOM_WALK_DIGEST__ matches the constant
playwright_chromium_e2e.significance: This is a meaningful subset of Phase 1.B's cross-runtime gate — real-browser cross-runtime determinism is now proven for chromium. Only firefox + webkit remain, which is what the CI workflow exists to verify.

fixes_landed_to_unblock_ci: playwright.config.ts: webServer command changed from `npm run build && npm run preview` to `npx vite preview --port 4173 --host 127.0.0.1`. Reasoning: the npm-run wrapper interfered with signal propagation in some shells, causing vite preview to die before Playwright connected. Bypassing `npm run` keeps the process tree shallow and reliable. The build step runs as a separate CI step now (see workflow change below).

.github/workflows/deploy.yml: reordered steps so `build` runs before `install playwright browsers` and `cross-runtime e2e`. Previously the build step ran AFTER e2e, which would have failed in CI because vite preview has nothing to serve. This was a latent bug in the Phase 1.A workflow that would have surfaced on first CI run.

what_the_host_must_still_do: Commit and push this branch to origin/master so the deploy workflow runs.

Confirm the workflow's `build & test` job is green end-to-end. Critical steps to watch: `npx playwright install --with-deps chromium firefox webkit` (the `--with-deps` flag installs system libs via apt — must succeed on ubuntu-24.04 runners), and `cross-runtime e2e` running all three engines.

Confirm the `deploy to GitHub Pages` job is green. Watch for `enablement: true` actually creating the Pages site on first run; if it fails with 403, the host must enable Pages manually in repo Settings → Pages → Source: GitHub Actions.
Hit https://porkchop.github.io/icefall/ (or the URL the deploy job reports) and confirm the diagnostic page renders the green 'self-test passed' banner.
When all four Phase 1.B acceptance criteria pass externally, write artifacts/phase-approval.json with phase = 'phase-1B' (or let the next agent loop iteration do so after observing the green badge).

verification_in_sandbox.build: vite build — 6.53 KB gzipped (well under 50 KB Phase 1 budget)

remaining_phase_1A_followups_unchanged: code-review #2: bump no-float-arithmetic invalid-fixture count beyond addendum minimum (currently 20, target ~25) before Phase 2 sim code lands.
code-review #8: assert encodeAction emits optional fields in strictly increasing tag order across all 2^k field-presence combinations.
suggested_commit_message: phase-1B: chromium e2e green locally; fix webServer + CI step order; still blocked on external push
